title: Ephemerantha usterii
intro: Ephemerantha usterii, also known as Uster's orchid, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ephemerantha usterii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ephemerantha usterii is often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are used to relieve symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
